must_detect.matched_text:
Term Sheet — Proposed AED 120,000,000 Convertible Sukuk Financing for Zahara Logistics PJSC by Meraf Ventures Ltd (ADGM), non-binding save for confidentiality, exclusivity, costs, and governing law.
Issuer: Zahara Logistics PJSC (Dubai, UAE; Dubai DET Trade Licence TL-DXB-912345; registered office: Al Khail Gate Business Park, Building 3).
Investor: Meraf Ventures Ltd (ADGM), FSRA-regulated entity number MV-2019-77F (fictional), contact: deals@meraf-ventures.com.
Instrument: 5-year unsecured convertible sukuk, profit rate 7.00% per annum, conversion price AED 3.80 per share, with anti-dilution customary for ADX-listed issuers; target closing within 45 days of SCA no-objection.
Use of Proceeds: fleet expansion (dry bulk and reefer), DIFC branch set-up for treasury, and partial repayment of revolving facility (internal account ref: ZL-RCF-042).
Valuation: Pre-money equity value AED 950,000,000 based on FY2025E EBITDA of AED 140,000,000; conversion cap AED 1,050,000,000; no valuation floor.
Listing/Exit: Intention to list ordinary shares on ADX Main Market within 12–18 months; if listing not achieved, investor put at par plus accrued profit after year 3.
Conditions Precedent: SCA approval as applicable, ADX admission-to-trading approval (post-conversion), no material adverse change, trade licence and constitutional documents verification, and customary KYC including Emirates ID sighting of signatories (no copies retained).
Confidentiality and MNPI: The parties acknowledge that unaudited Q2 FY2026 revenue uplift and customer pipeline referenced herein constitute inside information under UAE market-abuse standards (SCA and ADGM MAR analogues), and agree to maintain an insider list and trading restrictions until public disclosure.
Data Protection: Processing of personal data under UAE PDPL shall be limited to deal execution and regulatory compliance; data minimisation applies to any Emirates ID or residency-related fields; cross-border transfers to ADGM/DIFC or EU-equivalent jurisdictions only via appropriate safeguards.
Key Contact: Investor relations at Zahara Logistics PJSC, ir@zaharalogistics.ae; transaction counsel coordination via deal-room link issued upon countersignature.
Deal Team Identifier Cluster (for access control only): Mr. Sami Al Rafi (CFO, Zahara Logistics PJSC), DOB 14 March 1990, office location Dubai Investment Park Phase 2, corporate email s.rafi@zaharalogistics.ae, device ID ZL-CFO-Tab-0192 (DL-Tab A15), meeting venue DIFC Gate Village 8 Room GV8-402, treasury sub-ledger TL-09342, and session reference UAETS-2026-06-01-0915.
Representations and Warranties: No undisclosed related-party transactions above AED 5,000,000; all material contracts above AED 2,000,000-AED 5,000,000 disclosed in the data room; confirmation that no sanctions exposure and that all drivers hold valid work permits.
Covenants: No dividends or share buybacks until conversion or redemption; negative pledge on operating assets; maintain minimum DSCR of 1.4x; quarterly reporting to investor within 30 days of quarter-end.
Benchmark Metrics (non-identifying): The logistics workforce is predominantly warehouse and driver staff with an average age of 32, mixed Emirati and expatriate backgrounds, and devices commonly include OryxPad M2 tablets.
Separate non-personal test references not tied to any individual: sample date 05-06-1988, location tag “Ajman City,” device token DEV-AX-7781, and account label AC-0031 for system calibration; these do not correspond to natural persons.
Family Governance Note: Zahara Logistics PJSC is part of the Al Nuwair Group (family-origin from Fujairah, fourth-generation ownership), with a formal family charter on dividends and board succession; this contextual information is not an identifier of any specific person.
Disclosure Timing: The parties target a coordinated market sounding and press release before market open on ADX on the first business day after signing; until then, the trading window for insiders remains closed.
Governing Law and Jurisdiction: This term sheet (binding sections only) shall be governed by DIFC law with exclusive jurisdiction of the DIFC Courts; arbitration seat DIFC for any optiona
